R03--support--R04: R03's duty to 'first think very deeply and thoroughly step-by-step' and 'describe your careful and systematic plan' supplies the deliberative substance of the Plan stage in R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' workflow. By spelling out what good planning looks like, R03 makes that phase of the parent process actually followable rather than a bare label.
R05--support--R04: R05's scripted opener — start every feature with 'Let me research the codebase and create a plan before implementing' — is the entry trigger that launches R04's Research -> Plan sequence. It motivates and operationalizes the workflow by guaranteeing the agent enters it at the right point every time.
R06--support--R04: R06 fills in the Research phase of R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' spine: 'understand existing patterns and architecture through systematic file exploration' tells the agent concretely how to do the workflow's first step, making the parent process possible to execute.
R07--support--R04: R07 instantiates the Plan stage of R04's four-phase workflow: 'propose approach with architectural reasoning and verify with me if uncertain' specifies the deliverable (an approach plus reasoning) and an escape hatch (verify when uncertain) for that phase, so R04's abstract 'Plan' becomes actionable.
R08--refinement--R01: R08's 'build complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling' restates R01's broad mandate that all code be 'working, fully-functional ... and ready for production' in narrower, more concrete terms — it pins the umbrella quality bar to specific artifacts (tests, error handling) within the implementation phase.
R08--support--R04: R08 is the Implement phase of R04's 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' workflow: by stating what implementation must produce ('complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling'), it gives the third stage of the parent process its content.
R09--support--R04: R09 supplies the Validate phase of R04's workflow: 'ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation' is the concrete mechanism for the fourth stage, so the parent 'Research -> Plan -> Implement -> Validate' loop can actually close.
R09--checkpoint--R08: R09 mandates a verification moment — 'ALWAYS run formatters, linters, and tests after implementation' — that checks whether R08's 'complete, production-ready solutions with tests and error handling' were actually delivered. It adds no new build obligation; it only enforces that the implement-phase output meets its bar, the classic checkpoint pattern.
R11--support--R10: R10 says to 'stop, step back, and reflect on precisely what information you need from me' after three failed tries; R11 supplies the method for that reflection — 'intelligently gather information - ask for code output, files, context, or whatever else is necessary'. R11 tells the agent how to productively spend the stop that R10 mandates, making the 3-Try Rule effective rather than an empty pause.
R14--refinement--R13: R13 broadly orders 'add and verify logs to validate assumptions'; R14 turns this into a concrete stuck-state procedure: 'reflect on 5-7 possible sources, distill to 1-2 most likely, then add logs to validate before implementing the fix'. R14 narrows R13 by specifying when (when stuck), how many hypotheses, and the ordering (logs before fix).
R16--duplication--R36: R16's 'root cause analysis over quick fixes' states the same norm as R36's 'never debug just to get tests to pass - get at the root of the issue and fix underlying logic' — both privilege root-cause fixes over expedient patches. R16 adds no new trigger or obligation on this point, so the overlap is an ineffective restatement; arguably R16 is the softer preference-flavored copy (the annotator called it 'feels softer, super vague').
R20--refinement--R17: R17's general style preference for a 'professional yet direct tone, content-first with minimal fluff' is restated as a specific, actionable duty in R20: 'directly point out mistakes in reasoning rather than softening corrections'. R20 narrows 'direct' to one concrete behavior — unsoftened error correction — so it refines the broader tone rule.
R21--conflict--R23: R21 prohibits ending 'with opt-in follow-up suggestions unless specifically relevant', while R23 mandates ending every response with a template whose final block is 'Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions'. A compliant R23 footer routinely produces exactly the opt-in suggestions R21 forbids, so the two pull in opposite directions; R21's 'unless specifically relevant' carve-out softens but does not resolve the clash, since R23 requires suggestions unconditionally.
R27--refinement--R26: R26's abstract 'prioritize modularity' pillar is made concrete and testable by R27: 'keep functions small and focused - if you need comments to explain sections, split into functions'. R27 supplies an operational criterion (section comments signal a needed split) for achieving the modularity R26 demands.
R31--refinement--R26: R31 operationalizes the DRY pillar of R26 ('prioritize modularity, DRY, performance, and security') with a specific action: 'refactor repetitive logic into reusable functions or shared modules'. It narrows the slogan to one checkable practice, so it refines rather than merely echoes R26.
R33--refinement--R26: R33's single-responsibility rule — 'each function or module must do one thing well' — is a concrete facet of R26's 'prioritize modularity'. It converts the abstract modularity priority into a per-unit design constraint that can be checked on any function or module.
R34--refinement--R26: R34 refines R26's modularity pillar at the architectural level: 'UI logic, state management, and backend comms should be modular and decoupled. Avoid mixing layers' names the specific layers that must stay separate, turning R26's generic 'prioritize modularity' into a layer-separation requirement.
R36--duplication--R44: R36 ('never debug just to get tests to pass - get at the root of the issue and fix underlying logic') and R44 ('NEVER take the easy path or skip to an alternative the moment you encounter a bug; dig deep at the root') prohibit the same behavior — shortcut fixes instead of root-cause work — merely from two angles (test-gaming vs. plan-abandonment). Neither adds a distinct obligation over the other, so this is the same norm written twice.
R37--refinement--R26: R37 narrows R26's DRY pillar into a reuse-first policy: 'reuse existing functions before creating new ones. Only extend when clearly justified.' It specifies the concrete decision rule (check for existing functions, justify any extension) by which the abstract 'prioritize ... DRY' is satisfied.
R39--refinement--R26: R39's 'design logic as reusable, isolated components. No duplication. Think in shareable patterns' concretizes both the modularity and DRY pillars of R26 into a component-design directive. It is a refinement, though a weak one — it largely restates R26 at only slightly lower altitude and overlaps heavily with R31/R37, so it borders on duplication.
R40--refinement--R01: R01 broadly demands code that is 'working, fully-functional ... and ready for production'; R40 sharpens this into an explicit prohibition on the failure mode: 'production-ready code only - no placeholders, no maybe this works'. By naming what production-ready excludes (placeholders, unverified guesses), R40 makes the umbrella mandate concretely checkable.
R41--refinement--R26: R41 operationalizes R26's security pillar with mechanically checkable practices: 'always validate inputs, use secure randomness, prepared statements'. Each item is a specific implementation of 'prioritize ... security', turning the abstract priority into auditable requirements.
R45--conflict--R10: R45 commands 'you MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved', while R10 orders the opposite at a concrete trigger: 'if we fail to achieve our goal within three tries, stop, step back, and reflect'. After three failed attempts the agent cannot satisfy both 'keep going' and 'stop'. The conflict is arguably resolvable by reading R10's stop as a reflective pause inside R45's larger persistence loop, but as written the directives pull in opposite directions at try four.
C01--support_(defines)--R22: C01 is the literal template — 'Language > Specialist: {language} > {expert role}; Includes ...; Requirements ...; ## Plan' — that R22 orders the agent to 'start responses with'. Without this context node the mandate would be ambiguous; C01 defines the exact format that makes R22 followable and checkable.
C02--support_(defines)--R23: C02 spells out the end-of-response format R23 mandates: 'History: compressed summary ...; Source Tree: files with 💾/⚠️/👻 and ✅/⭕️/🔴 status; Next Task: next step or enhancement suggestions'. It defines the three blocks (including the exact status emojis) that give R23's 'end responses with History / Source Tree / Next Task' its concrete meaning — and incidentally its 'Next Task suggestions' clause is what puts R23 at odds with R21.
C03--support_(defines)--R10: C03's collaboration protocol — 'ALWAYS stop and ask when code depends on prior outputs; never assume code worked without proof' — articulates the working relationship that R10's 3-Try Rule instantiates: stopping and asking the user for 'precisely what information you need' is the protocol's stop-and-ask norm given a concrete three-failure trigger. The 'defines' variant is slightly loose here; C03 reads more like the rationale grounding R10 than a strict definition.
C03--support_(defines)--R11: C03 enumerates exactly what R11's 'intelligently gather information' means in practice: 'proactively ask for outputs/files/context; never assume structure or contents'. The context node defines the asking-over-assuming behavior that R11 prescribes, listing the concrete artifacts (outputs, files, context) the rule's 'ask for code output, files, context' refers to.
C04--support_(defines)--R45: C04 sets the quality bar — 'you may take multiple turns, but the final code must be perfect and production-ready' — that motivates R45's 'you MUST iterate and keep going until the problem is solved'. The explicit license to take multiple turns plus the perfection requirement is precisely why iteration-until-solved is mandated; 'motivates' would arguably fit better than 'defines', since C04 justifies rather than defines R45's terms.
C04--support_(defines)--R44: C04's demand to 'check rigorously; watch boundary/edge cases ... the final code must be perfect and production-ready' supplies the reason behind R44's prohibition on taking 'the easy path or skip[ping] to an alternative the moment you encounter a bug': easy-path fixes cannot meet a perfection bar. As with the R45 edge, this is closer to support-(motivates) than a strict definition, but the grounding link is real.
